reforms.description: The Mexican government has initiated measures to strengthen the microfinance sector, known as 'social banking', to improve access to financing for small enterprises, which are crucial for job creation and economic development. Additionally, initiatives have been launched to provide technological support to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) to enhance their management skills and production practices, particularly through the use of information and communications technologies (ICT). The National Committee for Productivity and Technological Innovation (COMPITE) was established to provide targeted counselling for micro-firms and SMEs, helping them improve productivity through efficient manufacturing methods.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures aim to enhance access to financing and technological support, which are essential for the growth and productivity of small enterprises.
reforms.status_evidence: The specific programme for technological development in SMEs has been implemented, and COMPITE has been established and operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at supporting SMEs through financial and technological means.
reforms.source_quote: The National Committee for Productivity and Technological Innovation (COMPITE) started up in 1997. It provides specifically targeted counselling for micro-firms and SMEs, aimed at helping them to apply more efficient manufacturing methods and so improve their productivity.
